event: 74 years old
white woman
presented to the Emergency Department
low-grade fever
dry cough
shortness of breath
admitted to another hospital for an elective right total knee replacement
uneventful post-operative course
denies any recent sick contacts or travel history
pain, redness, and swelling in the right knee
essential hypertension
obesity
myasthenia gravis in remission
osteoarthritis
blood pressure of 121/82
pulse of 87 beats per minute
respiratory rate of 16 breaths per minute
oxygen saturation of 87% while breathing ambient air
lung auscultation revealed bilateral rhonchi with rales
CXR was performed, which reported patchy air space opacity in the right upper lobe suspicious for pneumonia
temperature of 37.3°C
rapid nucleic acid amplification test (NAAT) for influenza A and B was negative
nasopharyngeal swab specimen was obtained and sent to the state laboratory for detection of SARS-CoV-2
admitted to the airborne-isolation unit
initially started on broad-spectrum antibiotics with cefepime and levofloxacin
mild diarrhea
generalized weakness
fatigue
evaluated by Neurology
started on 1 g/kg intravenous immunoglobulin for 4 days
nasopharyngeal swab results came back positive for SARSCoV-2 by reverse-transcriptase polymerase chain reaction (RTPCR)
started on oral hydroxychloroquine 400 mg once and started on 200 mg twice a day
along with azithromycin 500 mg once a day intravenously
zinc sulfate 220 mg 3 times a day
oral vitamin C 1 g twice a day
blood and sputum cultures did not grow any organisms
broad-spectrum antibiotics were discontinued
SOB worsened rapidly
oxygen requirements went up to 15 L
drowsy, in moderate distress
unable to protect the airways
blood pressure was 78/56 mmHg
heart rate of 112 beats per minute
temperature 38°C
respiratory rate of 28 breaths per minute
CXR revealed bilateral alveolar infiltrates due to pneumonia and interstitial edema
consistent with ARDS
intubated on an emergent basis
started on pressure-regulated volume-controlled mechanical ventilation
started on norepinephrine 0.02 mcg/kg/min
colchicine 0.6 mg twice a day
high-dose vita-min C 11 g per 24 h as a continuous intravenous infusion
clinical condition started to improve slowly
norepinephrine support was stopped
CXR on day 10 showed significant improvement of the pneumonia and interstitial edema
spontaneous breathing trial with CPAP/PS with the settings of PEEP of 7 mmHg
PS above PEEP of 10 mmHg
fraction of inspired oxygen of 40%
extubated to 4 L of oxygen with a nasal cannula
breathing status continued to improve
oxygen saturation of 92% on day 16 of illness while breathing ambient air
CXR revealed almost complete resolution of the infiltrates
received a total of 5 days of treatment with hydroxychloroquine and azithromycin
along with 4 days of colchicine
high-dose vitamin C infusion and oral zinc sulfate were continued for a total of 10 days
received inpatient physical and occupational rehabilitation
still positive by RT-PCR for SARS-CoV-2 on day 16 of illness
discharged from the hospital in stable condition with an additional 14 days of quarantine
